input:
Clinical Research Associate II
Ideaya Biosciences | South San Francisco, CA | Hybrid (4 days onsite)

Ideaya Biosciences is a precision oncology company focused on synthetic lethality therapeutics. We are seeking a CRA II to join our Clinical Operations team supporting Phase II/III trials in our PARP inhibitor and MAT2A inhibitor programs.

Responsibilities:
- Monitor clinical trial sites per ICH E6(R2) GCP guidelines
- Perform site qualification, initiation, monitoring, and closeout visits
- Review source documents and ensure data accuracy in Medidata Rave
- Track site metrics and escalate issues to the CTM
- Prepare visit reports within 5 business days

Requirements:
- 3+ years sponsor-side CRA experience; CRO experience will not be considered
- Oncology monitoring experience strongly preferred
- Proficiency with EDC systems (Medidata Rave preferred)
- Strong working knowledge of GCP, ICH, and 21 CFR Part 11
- BA/BS in life sciences
- Located in the San Francisco Bay Area; 4 days onsite required

This is a permanent position reporting to the Senior CTM.

Clinical Trial Manager
Karyopharm Therapeutics | Newton, MA | Onsite (3 days)

Karyopharm is a commercial-stage oncology company with marketed products (XPOVIO) and an active Phase II/III pipeline. We seek an experienced Clinical Trial Manager to lead operations for our hematologic malignancies portfolio.

Responsibilities:
- Serve as primary operational lead for 2-3 Phase II/III trials in myeloma and MDS
- Oversee CRO and vendor performance; manage study timelines and budgets
- Partner with medical monitors, biostatistics, and data management teams
- Lead cross-functional study teams; present at governance meetings
- Manage site activation milestones and enrollment forecasting

Requirements:
- 7+ years clinical development experience, including 3+ years sponsor-side CTM/CPM experience
- Direct Phase II/III experience in oncology or hematology required
- Experience managing CROs and clinical vendors
- Strong budget management and vendor oversight skills
- PMP or equivalent preferred
- Newton, MA office; 3 days per week onsite

Permanent role reporting to the Director of Clinical Operations.

Associate Director, Clinical Outsourcing
Apogee Therapeutics | Waltham, MA | Remote

Apogee Therapeutics is a clinical-stage immunology company developing next-generation biologics for atopic dermatitis, asthma, and related conditions. We are building our outsourcing function from the ground up and seeking an Associate Director to lead vendor strategy.

Responsibilities:
- Develop and manage the clinical outsourcing strategy for Phase II/III programs
- Lead CRO selection, contracting, and performance oversight
- Build preferred provider network across CROs, central labs, eClinical vendors, and specialty service providers
- Draft and negotiate MSAs, work orders, and change orders
- Track vendor budget adherence and SLA performance
- Partner with Finance on clinical spend forecasting

Requirements:
- 8+ years clinical development experience; 4+ years in clinical outsourcing or alliance management at a biotech/pharma sponsor
- Experience building or managing an outsourcing function at a sponsor company
- Deep knowledge of CRO contracting, scope negotiation, and performance management
- Immunology or respiratory therapeutic area experience a plus
- Remote role; must be able to travel 15-20% for vendor meetings
- BA/BS required; MBA or advanced degree a plus

This is a permanent role reporting to the VP, Clinical Operations.

Medical Director, Pharmacovigilance
Insmed Incorporated | Bridgewater, NJ | Hybrid (2 days onsite)

Insmed is a global biopharmaceutical company focused on rare disease, with marketed products (ARIKAYCE, BRENSOCATIB) and late-stage pipeline assets. We seek a Medical Director to lead pharmacovigilance medical strategy for our rare pulmonary disease portfolio.

Responsibilities:
- Provide medical oversight for serious adverse event (SAE) review, case causality assessments, and signal detection
- Author and review ICSRs, PSURs, PBRERs, and DSURs
- Lead safety governance committee for 2 marketed products and 2 clinical-stage compounds
- Represent PV medical affairs in regulatory agency interactions (FDA, EMA)
- Collaborate with clinical development, regulatory affairs, and medical affairs teams

Requirements:
- MD degree required; board certification in an internal medicine subspecialty preferred
- 5+ years of drug safety/pharmacovigilance experience in biotech/pharma (sponsor or CRO experience both valued)
- Expertise with MedDRA coding and signal management tools (Argus, Oracle)
- Experience preparing agency submissions and responding to health authority queries
- Rare disease or pulmonary experience a plus
- Bridgewater, NJ office; 2 days per week onsite

Permanent position reporting to the VP, Drug Safety & Pharmacovigilance.

Associate Director, Drug Safety Operations
Structure Therapeutics | San Francisco, CA | Remote

Structure Therapeutics is a late-stage clinical biopharmaceutical company developing oral GLP-1 receptor agonists for obesity and metabolic disease. We are preparing for NDA submission and seek an Associate Director to build our drug safety operations function.

Responsibilities:
- Oversee end-to-end individual case safety report (ICSR) processing and submission
- Manage PV vendors and safety database (Argus/Oracle) operations
- Author and maintain the Company Core Safety Information (CCSI) and Investigator Brochure safety sections
- Develop SOPs for expedited and periodic safety reporting
- Coordinate with regulatory affairs on IND safety reporting requirements under 21 CFR 312.32
- Support NDA safety section preparation

Requirements:
- 7+ years drug safety/pharmacovigilance experience; sponsor or CRO background both considered
- Hands-on Argus database experience strongly preferred
- Experience with both clinical-stage and post-marketing PV activities
- NDA or BLA submission experience highly desirable
- Metabolic disease, obesity, or cardiometabolic TA experience a plus
- Fully remote; US-based

Permanent role, reporting to the VP, Drug Safety.

Director, Biostatistics
Regeneron Pharmaceuticals | Tarrytown, NY | Hybrid (3 days onsite)

Regeneron is a leading science-driven biopharmaceutical company. We seek a Director of Biostatistics to provide senior statistical leadership for our rare disease and ophthalmology clinical programs.

Responsibilities:
- Serve as lead statistician on Phase III registration trials and NDA/BLA submissions
- Author and review statistical analysis plans (SAPs), randomization schemes, and TFL shells
- Represent biostatistics on cross-functional study teams and in FDA meetings
- Mentor junior statisticians and statistical programmers
- Drive methodology innovation: adaptive designs, estimand frameworks, Bayesian approaches

Requirements:
- PhD in Biostatistics or Statistics required
- 8+ years pharmaceutical/biotech industry experience; sponsor or CRO background both considered
- Regulatory submission (NDA/BLA) experience required
- Proficiency in SAS and/or R; experience with CDISC ADaM standards
- Experience with rare disease orphan drug development or ophthalmology a plus
- Tarrytown, NY office; 3 days per week onsite

Permanent role reporting to the VP, Biostatistics.

Statistical Programmer II / Senior Statistical Programmer
Vertex Pharmaceuticals | Boston, MA | Remote

Vertex is a global biotechnology company focused on transformative medicines for serious diseases including cystic fibrosis, sickle cell disease, and pain. We seek a Statistical Programmer to support our expanding clinical pipeline.

Responsibilities:
- Develop and validate CDISC-compliant SDTM and ADaM datasets for clinical studies
- Generate and validate TFLs for clinical study reports and regulatory submissions
- Contribute to define.xml and reviewer's guide documentation
- Support FDA and EMA submission programming activities
- Collaborate with biostatistics and data management teams

Requirements:
- 4+ years clinical statistical programming experience (sponsor or CRO background valued)
- Strong SAS macro programming skills required; R a plus
- CDISC SDTM and ADaM standards expertise
- NDA, BLA, or MAA submission experience preferred
- Experience with Pinnacle 21 validation tools
- Rare disease or gene therapy TA experience a plus
- Remote-eligible; US-based

Permanent position.

Senior Clinical Data Manager
BioMarin Pharmaceutical | San Rafael, CA | Hybrid (2 days onsite)

BioMarin is a global leader in rare disease therapeutics with 7 marketed products and a robust pipeline. We seek a Senior CDM to lead data management activities for our enzyme replacement therapy and gene therapy programs.

Responsibilities:
- Serve as lead CDM on Phase I/II gene therapy and Phase III rare disease trials
- Build and maintain clinical databases in Medidata Rave
- Author CRF design, data management plans, data validation specifications, and edit checks
- Oversee external data vendors (central labs, ePRO, cardiac safety)
- Perform CDISC SDTM mapping and data transfer to biostatistics
- Lead database lock activities

Requirements:
- 6+ years clinical data management experience; sponsor or CRO background considered
- Medidata Rave expertise required; Veeva Vault EDC experience a plus
- CDISC CDASH and SDTM standards knowledge
- Rare disease, gene therapy, or orphan drug experience strongly preferred
- San Rafael, CA area preferred; hybrid 2 days onsite

Permanent position reporting to the Director, Clinical Data Management.

Director, Clinical Data Management
Sarepta Therapeutics | Cambridge, MA | Onsite (4 days)

Sarepta is a commercial-stage rare neuromuscular disease company with marketed gene therapies (ELEVIDYS) and an active pipeline. We seek a Director of CDM to lead the data management organization for our rare disease clinical portfolio.

Responsibilities:
- Lead a team of 6 CDMs and contractors supporting Phase II/III and post-marketing studies
- Oversee EDC build, CRF standards, and data governance across 8 active studies
- Drive CDISC CDASH/SDTM standardization initiatives
- Manage CDM vendor relationships and budgets
- Partner with Biostatistics, Regulatory Affairs, and Clinical Operations on data deliverables
- Present CDM metrics to senior leadership

Requirements:
- 10+ years CDM experience including 3+ years in people management at a biotech/pharma sponsor
- Expertise in Medidata Rave or Veeva Vault EDC
- CDISC standards expertise (CDASH, SDTM)
- Rare disease, neuromuscular, or gene therapy experience preferred
- Experience managing CDM vendor relationships and SOW negotiations
- Cambridge, MA office; 4 days per week onsite required

Permanent role reporting to the VP, Data Sciences.

VP, Clinical Development
bluebird bio | Somerville, MA | Hybrid (2 days onsite)

bluebird bio is a clinical-stage gene therapy company focused on severe genetic diseases. We seek a VP of Clinical Development to lead the clinical strategy for our beta-thalassemia and sickle cell disease programs through regulatory approval.

Responsibilities:
- Serve as Clinical Program Lead for ZYNTEGLO (approved) and pipeline hemoglobinopathy assets
- Design and oversee Phase II/III clinical trials including endpoint selection, protocol design, and investigator meetings
- Lead interactions with FDA, EMA, and other health authorities on clinical strategy
- Build and lead a team of medical directors and clinical scientists
- Partner with regulatory affairs, CMC, and commercial teams on BLA/MAA lifecycle management

Requirements:
- MD or MD/PhD required
- 10+ years pharmaceutical/biotech industry experience, with 5+ years in clinical development leadership
- Hematology, gene therapy, or rare disease therapeutic area experience required
- NDA/BLA/MAA submission and approval experience
- Experience managing cross-functional clinical teams and agency interactions
- Somerville, MA preferred; hybrid schedule considered

Permanent executive role reporting to the Chief Medical Officer.

Medical Director, Clinical Development – Oncology
Gilead Sciences | Foster City, CA | Onsite (4 days)

Gilead Sciences is a research-based biopharmaceutical company developing innovative medicines in oncology, HIV, and inflammation. We seek a Medical Director to lead clinical development for our antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) oncology pipeline.

Responsibilities:
- Serve as clinical program lead for 2 ADC assets in Phase I/II solid tumor trials
- Design Phase I dose-escalation and expansion protocols; lead DSMB interactions
- Evaluate emerging clinical data and drive go/no-go decisions with the CMO
- Represent clinical development in KOL engagement, advisory boards, and medical conference presentations
- Contribute to IND, CTA, and NDA/BLA preparation

Requirements:
- MD required; hematology/oncology board certification strongly preferred
- 6+ years oncology clinical development experience in biotech/pharma
- ADC, IO, or targeted therapy clinical development experience preferred
- Phase I study design and DSMB experience
- Foster City, CA office; 4 days onsite expected

Permanent role reporting to the SVP, Clinical Development.

Vice President, Medical Affairs
Moderna | Cambridge, MA | Hybrid (3 days onsite)

Moderna is a commercial-stage biopharma company with a broad mRNA pipeline spanning infectious disease, oncology, and rare disease. We seek a VP of Medical Affairs to lead the global medical strategy for our oncology franchise.

Responsibilities:
- Build and lead the medical affairs function for Moderna's oncology portfolio (mRNA cancer vaccines, IL-2 programs)
- Develop global medical plans, publication strategies, and evidence generation frameworks
- Lead field medical teams (MSLs) and advisory board strategy
- Establish KOL partnerships across academic oncology centers
- Partner with clinical development, regulatory, and commercial teams on launch readiness
- Represent medical affairs to executive leadership and the Board

Requirements:
- MD, PharmD, or PhD required
- 12+ years biopharmaceutical industry experience, with 6+ years in medical affairs leadership
- Oncology therapeutic area expertise required; immuno-oncology preferred
- Experience leading global medical affairs teams (10+ FTEs)
- Track record of successful product launches and KOL development
- Cambridge, MA office; 3 days per week onsite

Permanent senior executive role reporting to the Chief Medical Officer.

Chief Medical Officer
Alexion Pharmaceuticals (AstraZeneca Rare Disease) | Boston, MA | Remote with quarterly travel

Alexion, an AstraZeneca Rare Disease company, seeks a Chief Medical Officer to lead the global medical organization across our complement biology, neurology, and metabolic rare disease portfolios.

Responsibilities:
- Serve as the senior medical leader overseeing clinical development, medical affairs, regulatory medical, and pharmacovigilance
- Drive pipeline strategy for 15+ active programs spanning Phase I through post-marketing
- Lead interactions with FDA, EMA, and PMDA on critical regulatory decisions
- Build and develop a medical organization of 80+ FTEs across global offices
- Represent Alexion's medical strategy to the AstraZeneca Board, investors, and KOL community
- Champion patient advocacy relationships in the rare disease community

Requirements:
- MD required; subspecialty training in neurology, nephrology, hematology, or rare disease preferred
- 15+ years biopharmaceutical industry experience; 8+ years in C-suite or equivalent senior medical leadership
- Track record overseeing multiple NDA/BLA/MAA approvals
- Global clinical development organization leadership (50+ FTEs)
- Complement biology, rare neurology, or rare metabolic disease experience strongly preferred
- Boston-based preferred; flexible remote for the right candidate with quarterly travel expectations

This is a permanent C-suite executive appointment.

Associate Director / Director, Regulatory Affairs CMC
AbbVie | North Chicago, IL | Onsite (5 days)

AbbVie is a global biopharmaceutical leader with a broad portfolio spanning immunology, oncology, neuroscience, and aesthetics. We seek an Associate Director or Director of Regulatory Affairs CMC to support our biologics pipeline.

Responsibilities:
- Lead CMC regulatory strategy for 2 biologics IND/NDA programs
- Author and review CMC sections of INDs, NDAs, BLAs, and supplements
- Interact with FDA Office of Pharmaceutical Quality (OPQ) on CMC matters
- Support manufacturing changes, post-approval supplements, and lifecycle management
- Partner with CMC development, quality, and manufacturing teams

Requirements:
- 8+ years regulatory affairs experience with strong CMC focus in biologics
- Experience preparing BLA/NDA CMC modules (Module 3)
- Working knowledge of ICH Q guidelines (Q8-Q11) and FDA/EMA CMC expectations
- Monoclonal antibody or bispecific antibody manufacturing experience preferred
- North Chicago, IL; fully onsite 5 days per week required

Permanent role reporting to the VP, Regulatory Affairs.

Contract Clinical Research Associate – Oncology (6-Month Contract, Immediate Start)
[Confidential Client] | Remote (US-Based) | Contract

GQR Global Markets is engaged by a confidential Phase II/III oncology biotech to identify an experienced Contract CRA for immediate deployment. This is a 6-month contract with strong extension potential.

Responsibilities:
- Independent monitoring of 4-6 oncology clinical trial sites (Phase II/III solid tumor trial)
- Remote monitoring plus 1-2 onsite visits per site per quarter
- Source document verification, data cleaning follow-up, and protocol deviation management
- Site metric reporting and escalation to the sponsor CTM
- ICF review and regulatory binder maintenance

Requirements:
- 4+ years CRA experience; sponsor-side experience required; CRO monitoring experience does not qualify
- Solid tumor or hematology oncology protocol experience required
- Medidata Rave or equivalent EDC proficiency
- CCRC or ACRP certification preferred
- Available to start within 2 weeks
- US-based; fully remote with travel to sites

Competitive contract day rate. Team account at GQR — Bryan Blair and the GQR oncology team are your recruiting contacts. Submission speed is critical; first qualified CV presented to client within 24 hours.
